metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Acknowledge in the Scope section that `overlap_enabled = true` also activates the opportunistic enrichment drain (in `src/rebar/llm/enrich_drain.py`) on ticket write paths, not only the plan-review overlap step.
findings.finding: The plan states the flag is 'consumed in the plan-review path' but omits that `src/rebar/llm/enrich_drain.py` is a second, independent consumer: flipping `overlap_enabled = true` also activates the opportunistic enrichment drain on EVERY ticket write path (push/status writes), not just during plan review. This is a distinct behavioral side-effect the plan does not name or acknowledge.
findings.location: Scope section: 'consumed in the plan-review path (`src/rebar/_config_schema.py`)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the Scope section: 'Note: `overlap_enabled` also gates the opportunistic enrichment drain (`src/rebar/llm/enrich_drain.py`), which runs asynchronously on ticket write paths to build BM25F digests. This is intentional — overlap detection requires enriched digests. LLM calls from the drain are subject to `[tool.rebar.llm] overlap_drain` tunables.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC that exercises the newly-enabled overlap path end-to-end: e.g., run `rebar plan-review` against a known-overlapping fixture and assert that `overlap[]` is non-empty (or that the overlap step appears in the review output), proving the flag is read and the detector runs.
findings.finding: The plan enables a new code path (store-wide overlap detection) by flipping `overlap_enabled = true`, but no acceptance criterion exercises that path end-to-end to confirm it actually runs and surfaces `overlap[]` suggestions as claimed. All ACs only verify the config key is set and lint passes — they do not prove the new behavior executes.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria (all four ACs); Scope section: 'consumed in the plan-review path'
findings.suggested_fix: Add AC: 'Running `rebar plan-review --project . <fixture_plan>` with the updated config produces output containing at least one `overlap[]` entry (or logs the overlap-check step), confirming the detector is active.' If a full fixture is unavailable, at minimum add a config-parsing smoke test: `python -c "import tomllib; cfg=tomllib.load(open('rebar.toml','rb')); assert cfg['verify']['overlap_enabled'] is True"` and a separate invocation-level check.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State a concrete parse-proving command for AC2, e.g., `python -c "import tomllib; tomllib.load(open('rebar.toml','rb'))"`  (Python ≥3.11) or `python -m tomllib rebar.toml`, so parse validity is deterministically evidenced.
findings.finding: AC2 asserts 'the file still parses (local build, not stale global)' but provides no concrete proving command, leaving parse-validity unverified by a deterministic check.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC2: 'No other config keys change; the file still parses (local build, not stale global).'
findings.suggested_fix: Append to AC2: 'Verified by `python -c "import tomllib; tomllib.load(open(\"rebar.toml\",\"rb\"))"` exiting 0.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a one-sentence positive rationale in the Scope explaining why `rebar.toml` is the correct location (e.g., it is the tracked/shared project config preferred by config discovery, ensuring the flag is persistent and shared across all contributors).
findings.finding: The plan specifies what to edit (`rebar.toml` `[verify]` section) but provides no positive rationale for *why* `rebar.toml` is the correct config location — e.g., that it is the tracked/shared project config preferred by config discovery, making enablement persistent and repo-wide for all contributors. Without this, a reader cannot evaluate whether an env var or an alternative config file would be equally valid.
findings.suggested_fix: In the Scope section, add: "`rebar.toml` is the tracked, git-committed project config preferred by rebar's config discovery over the git-ignored `.rebar/config.conf`; editing it ensures the flag is persistent and shared for all contributors without per-machine env-var setup."

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
